perturbation.original_text: Unless earlier terminated as provided below, this Agreement shall remain in effect until the later to occur of the date that is (x) three (3) years from the date hereof and (y) the date on which an affiliate of Freedom Mortgage is not acting as the external manager of Cherry Hill.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless earlier terminated as provided below, this Agreement shall remain in effect until the earlier to occur of the date that is (x) three (3) years from the date hereof and (y) the date on which an affiliate of Freedom Mortgage is not acting as the external manager of Cherry Hill.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'later' to 'earlier' creates an in-text contradiction regarding the term of the agreement. Originally, the agreement lasted until the later of two dates, effectively extending the contract. Now, it terminates at the earlier of the two, shortening it. This directly contradicts the intent of extending the contract based on the manager's affiliation and introduces uncertainty about the agreement's duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless earlier terminated as provided below, this Agreement shall remain in effect until the earlier to occur of the date that is (x) three (3) years from the date hereof and (y) the date on which an affiliate of Freedom Mortgage is not acting as the external manager of Cherry Hill.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'later' to 'earlier' in the Changed Text directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, even though they are located in the same section. The original wording extends the contract's term, while the changed wording shortens it. This creates a clear and significant inconsistency regarding the agreement's duration, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes, as outlined in the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that a party materially breaches any representation or covenant herein, the other party may give written notice of the breach requiring the same to be remedied within 30 days of receipt of such notice. If the breaching party fails to remedy the material breach in such time period, the non-breaching party may terminate this Agreement by delivery of a written termination notice to the breaching party. Any such termination shall not relieve the breaching party from any obligation or liability arising prior to such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that a party materially breaches any representation or covenant herein, the other party may give written notice of the breach requiring the same to be remedied within 10 days of receipt of such notice. If the breaching party fails to remedy the material breach in such time period, the non-breaching party may, at its sole discretion, choose to either (a) continue the agreement with no recourse, (b) demand immediate payment of all anticipated profits for the remaining term of the agreement, or (c) terminate this Agreement by delivery of a written termination notice to the breaching party. Any such termination shall relieve the breaching party from any obligation or liability arising prior to such termination.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the remedy period from 30 days to 10 days creates an inconsistency. The original text specifies a clear procedure for breach resolution, while the modified text provides the non-breaching party with significantly altered and harsher options, including demanding payment of anticipated profits, instead of continuing the agreement. More specifically, the original stated that 'Any such termination shall not relieve the breaching party from any obligation or liability arising prior to such termination.', while the new text says 'Any such termination shall relieve the breaching party from any obligation or liability arising prior to such termination'. This creates uncertainty and contradiction around breach-related penalties and termination terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that a party materially breaches any representation or covenant herein, the other party may give written notice of the breach requiring the same to be remedied within 10 days of receipt of such notice. If the breaching party fails to remedy the material breach in such time period, the non-breaching party may, at its sole discretion, choose to either (a) continue the agreement with no recourse, (b) demand immediate payment of all anticipated profits for the remaining term of the agreement, or (c) terminate this Agreement by delivery of a written termination notice to the breaching party. Any such termination shall relieve the breaching party from any obligation or liability arising prior to such termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction regarding the consequences of termination following a material breach. The original text states that termination 'shall not relieve the breaching party from any obligation or liability arising prior to such termination,' while the changed text states that termination 'shall relieve the breaching party from any obligation or liability arising prior to such termination.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and changed text cannot both be true simultaneously; therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be amended except by an instrument in writing signed on behalf of each of the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended by (i) an instrument in writing signed on behalf of each of the Parties, or (ii) a verbal agreement recorded and transcribed by a mutually agreed-upon notary public.
perturbation.explanation: By including (ii), it introduces an in-text contradiction because the agreement now contains two conflicting methods for making amendments - one that requires a signed written instrument, and another that allows a verbal agreement. This directly contradicts the initial requirement for a written amendment and introduces ambiguity about which method is valid, creating uncertainty and potential disputes over the enforceability of any amendments made verbally.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties may (i) amend this Agreement, (ii) extend the time for the performance of any of the obligations or other acts of any other Party, (iii) waive any inaccuracies in the representations and warranties contained herein or in any document delivered pursuant hereto, or (iv) waive compliance with any of the agreements or conditions contained herein. This Agreement may be amended by (i) an instrument in writing signed on behalf of each of the Parties, or (ii) a verbal agreement recorded and transcribed by a mutually agreed-upon notary public. Any agreement on the part of a Party to any extension or waiver shall be valid only if set forth in an instrument in writing signed on behalf of such Party, but such waiver or failure to insist on strict compliance with such obligation, covenant, agreement or condition shall not operate as a waiver of, or estoppel with respect to, any subsequent or other failure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause allowing verbal agreements to amend the contract, which directly contradicts the existing clause requiring written instruments for amendments, as well as an existing requirement in the contradicted text for any waiver to be set forth in a written instrument. This creates an in-text contradiction as the contract now allows for two conflicting methods of amendment, creating ambiguity about which method is valid.
